FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, 21 recruitment sites, baseline ages 9-10 years, N=11,876

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: longitudinal cross-lagged structural equation panel models over 4 waves (baseline to year 3)

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed journal article (JAMA Network Open, May 2025)

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1 success: 'time spent on social media' (phone/SM-specific IV, measured as hours per day) paired with 'depressive symptoms' (CBCL raw score, individual-trait-level DV). Cross-lagged paths show SM→depression (β=0.07, p=0.01 year 1-2; β=0.09, p<0.001 year 2-3)

FT_IC3.evidence: Longitudinal cross-lagged regression β coefficients with 95% CI and p-values reported (β=0.07, CI 0.01-0.12; β=0.09, CI 0.04-0.14)

FT_IC4.evidence: Quantitative results: standardized β, 95% CI, p-values, and model fit indices (CFI, TLI, RMSEA)

FT_EC7_reason: Bidirectional cross-lagged design: SM is BOTH IV and DV in the same model (not DV-only). EC7 requires SM as DV in every analysis with no SM-as-IV anywhere; this paper has SM as IV. Condition 3 fails.
notes: Paper 8: Strong longitudinal design with within-person and between-person decomposition. Final model chosen for theoretical merit of time-specific associations. INCLUDE.

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, 21 sites, baseline ages 9-10 years, N=9,243 after exclusions

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: linear regression and mediation models testing prospective associations across Year 1, Year 2, Year 3

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed journal article (Social Psychiatry and Psychiatric Epidemiology, 2025)

FT_IC2.evidence: Step 1 success: Multiple phone/SM-specific IV measured in Year 1: 'social media' time (B=0.20), 'texting' time (B=0.18), 'videos' time (B=0.14), 'video games' time (B=0.09), all paired with 'manic symptoms' (7 Up subscale, individual-trait outcome) measured in Year 3

FT_IC3.evidence: Linear regression B coefficients with 95% CI and p-values: overall screen time B=0.05 (95% CI 0.03-0.07, p<0.001); social media B=0.20 (95% CI 0.09-0.32, p=0.001)

FT_IC4.evidence: Quantitative results: B coefficients, 95% CI, p-values, and indirect effects with bootstrap CI reported for mediation models

FT_EC7_reason: Longitudinal design (Year 1 predictor → Year 3 outcome) but screen time variables are IV, manic symptoms is DV (not SM as DV-only). Condition 2 fails. EC7 does not apply.
notes: Paper 49: Prospective analysis with mediation pathways through problematic SM use, problematic video game use, and sleep duration. INCLUDE.

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, Year 3 follow-up (2019-2021), ages 11-14 years, N=9,989

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis: logistic regression of cross-sectional data from ABCD Year 3

FT_IC2.evidence: No qualifying smartphone/SM variable on either side. Exposure: transgender or gender-questioning identity (individual-trait variable). Outcome: cyberbullying victimization (not SM/phone use; cyberbullying alone fails per BINDING clarification §2.4). No SM time, SM platform use, texting, video chat, app use, or problematic-use scales reported. Cyberbullying is not a phone/SM variable; it fails IC2 without separately-measured phone/SM variable paired.

notes: Paper 81: Cross-sectional analysis of gender identity and cyberbullying victimization. No SM/phone exposure variable. EXCLUDE: FT-EC2 (no qualifying SM/phone variable).
